properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: One lowercase hex character for a nibble in 0..15.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Encode a byte list as a lowercase hex String, two chars per byte.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Rotate a 32-bit word right by `n` bits. `x` is assumed already
masked to 32 bits; `(x << (32 - n))` can reach 2^63 only if x had
high bits, so the whole expression is re-masked to 32 bits.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Logical right shift of an already-masked 32-bit word.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
The 64 round constants (first 32 bits of the fractional parts of
the cube roots of the first 64 primes).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Read one 32-bit big-endian word from `block` starting at byte `i`.
Each byte is in 0..255; the top byte is masked to 8 bits before
the 24-bit shift so the product stays well under i64.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Build the 64-word message schedule for one 64-byte block.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
One block of the compression function. Returns the updated state
(the eight working words added back into the incoming state).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA padding: append 0x80, then zeros, then the 64-bit big-endian
bit length, to a multiple of 64 bytes. Returns a fresh byte list.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Run the full hash over `message` starting from `iv`, returning the
eight 32-bit state words.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Serialize `count` state words as big-endian bytes (4 each).

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-256 digest of `message` as 32 raw bytes.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-256 digest of `message` as a 64-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: SHA-256 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-224 digest of `message` as 28 raw bytes (the first seven
state words; the eighth is dropped).
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
SHA-224 digest of `message` as a 56-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: SHA-224 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Normalize a key to exactly 64 bytes: hash it if it is longer than
the block, then zero-pad to the block size.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: XOR every byte of a 64-byte block with a constant pad byte.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value: Concatenate two byte lists into a fresh list.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
HMAC-SHA256 of `message` under `key`, as 32 raw bytes.
BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `key` and `message` must be in
0..255. Out-of-range elements are silently masked to 8 bits
(`& 0xFF`: 256 -> 0, -1 -> 255), with no error, identically on
both backends. For text, prefer the `_utf8` wrapper or
`String.bytes()`, which are always in range.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
HMAC-SHA256 of `message` under `key`, as lowercase hex.
BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `key` and `message` must be in
0..255; out-of-range elements are silently masked to 8 bits
(`& 0xFF`), with no error, identically on both backends.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
HMAC-SHA256 over the UTF-8 bytes of a String key and message, as
lowercase hex.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Compare two byte lists in constant time, returning whether they are
equal. Differing lengths return false immediately (a tag's length
is not secret). For equal lengths, every byte pair is XORed and the
differences are OR-accumulated across the WHOLE list with no
short-circuit, so the running time depends only on the length, not
on where (or whether) the two lists first differ.

BYTE CONTRACT: each element of `a` and `b` should be in 0..255, as
produced by the hash functions here or by `String.bytes()`. Out-of-
range elements are masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`) before comparison,
identically on both backends, so e.g. 256 and 0 compare equal.

properties.name: capa:doc
properties.value:
Compare two strings for equality in constant time over their UTF-8
bytes. Intended for tags carried as text: two HMAC hex digests, two
one-time codes, two base64 tokens. Delegates to `bytes_equal` after
`.bytes()`; the time depends only on the byte length, not on the
position of the first difference.
